intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant named Calceolus candidus. However, I think you may be referring to Calceolus, also known as the "pretty violet" or more commonly as the  "Pansy" or the "Viollet". It is actually Calceolaria  candida. 

Here's a paragraph about Calceolaria candida:

 Calceolaria candida, also known as Slipper Flower, is a flowering plant in the family Calceolariaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in reducing stress and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Calceolaria candida include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calceolaria candida include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat gastrointestinal issues and promote relaxation.
